name: Listicle / Top-N
description: Numbered list video — 'Top 5 X you didn't know', '3 things to stop doing', '7 tools that replace your job'. 30-60s, 9:16, vertical. 0-2s hook + promise, then 6-10s per item with a big on-screen counter ('1/5', '2/5'), with #1 saved for the end as an open loop. Search-friendly evergreen format, AI-friendly (★★★ in research-doc table), YouTube Shorts is the primary platform.

stackSummary.keyframes: google/gemini-3-pro-image-preview via OpenRouter — one keyframe per list item (visual that anchors that item: object, scene, screenshot mockup, environment).
stackSummary.i2v: kwaivgi/kling-v3.0-pro via OpenRouter — short 4-5s b-roll loops per item, generate_audio: false. Loops should idle (subtle camera drift, ambient motion) so the on-screen counter and item text dominate attention.
stackSummary.voiceover: ElevenLabs eleven_multilingual_v2 — single continuous VO take across all items, ~one item per ~7s, energetic-but-clear delivery (faceless host pacing, not GRWM-soft, not rant-loud).
stackSummary.music: ElevenLabs Music — one steady-tempo or building-urgency bed underneath the whole video, with a small lift at #1 reveal. No abrupt cuts.
stackSummary.captions: OpenRouter whisper-1 + a Remotion-rendered numbered counter overlay (1/5, 2/5, …) plus an item-name title. Counter is the dominant visual element, not the captions.

constants.structure: 0-2s hook + promise → N item beats (6-10s each) → #1 reveal at end → CTA
constants.counterStyle: big-fraction-counter (e.g. '1/5'), top-center or top-left, monospace heavy weight

variationAxes: list size (3, 5, 7, 10) — 5 is the sweet spot for 45-60s
reveal order (reverse-countdown #5→#1 — default; or forward #1→#5 with #1 teased twice)
presence (faceless VO+b-roll — default; or face-on-camera host cutting between items)
niche (tech / AI tools, finance / money, food / recipes, travel / destinations, gaming, productivity)
VO language (any — English, Russian, Spanish; counter and on-screen number text are universal)
doNotCopyLiterally: Items, hook wording, exact prompts, music bed. Vibe and structure only.
referenceNotes: No user reference required by default — items are usually generic (tools, foods, places, habits). If a specific brand or named entity appears as an item (e.g. 'Notion', 'Tokyo's Shibuya Crossing'), the reference-required gate (AGENTS.md hard rule #3) re-applies for that single item only — supply a logo / photo, or rephrase the item generically ('a popular note app').
